2606.24007.title: Fast and Slow Variational Continual Learning
2606.24007.paper_summary: Continual learning remains a major challenge for modern deep networks, partly because commonly used optimizers lack inherent mechanisms for continual adaptation. One such natural mechanism is fast and slow adaptation to balance stability and plasticity. This mechanism has deep roots in neuroscience and biology, but there is no consensus on how to best incorporate it in commonly used optimizers. Here, we show that this can be easily done via the VCL framework, where past posteriors are used as priors in the future. Our key idea is to incorporate slow adaptation via merging of past posteriors to slow down the drift in the knowledge as learning progresses. The merged posterior is then used as the prior in the VCL update to implement the fast-weight updates. These steps can be seamlessly implemented in the IVON optimizer, whose form and costs are nearly identical to that of Adam. We call this new optimizer the Continual IVON (CoVON) optimizer and show that it not only consistently improves over existing VCL optimizers, but also performs better than other weight-regularization strategies across domain-incremental learning, continual pre-training, and fine-tuning of large language models.
2606.24007.paper_summary_zh: 持續學習仍然是現代深度網絡面臨的一個重大挑戰，部分原因是常用的優化器缺乏內在的持續適應機制。<lb/>其中一種自然機制是快速和緩慢適應，以平衡穩定性和可塑性。<lb/>這一機制在神經科學和生物學中有著深厚的根基，但對於如何最佳地將其納入常用的優化器尚無共識。<lb/>在這裡，我們展示了這可以通過 VCL 框架輕鬆實現，過去的後驗分佈被用作未來的先驗分佈。<lb/>我們的關鍵思想是通過合併過去的後驗分佈來納入緩慢適應，以減緩隨著學習進展而發生的知識漂移。<lb/>合併後的後驗分佈然後被用作 VCL 更新中的先驗分佈，以實現快速權重更新。<lb/>這些步驟可以無縫地實現在 IVON 優化器中，其形式和成本與 Adam 幾乎相同。<lb/>我們將這個新優化器稱為持續 IVON (CoVON) 優化器，並展示它不僅在性能上始終優於現有的 VCL 優化器，還在領域增量學習、持續預訓練和大型語言模型的微調中表現得比其他權重正則化策略更好。
2606.24007.author: Subarnaduti Paul et.al.
2606.24007.authors: Subarnaduti Paul, Yohan Jung, Mohammad Emtiyaz Khan, Siddharth Swaroop, Thomas Möllenhoff, Martin Mundt
